arguments.content:
# Sprint 8 — Week 1 Sprint Review (Presentation Outline)

Date: **2026-03-27 (Fri)**  
Sprint window: **Week 1 (2026-03-23 → 2026-03-27)**

---

## 1) Meeting goals
- Confirm Week 1 scope delivered vs. planned
- Review product/engineering outcomes and key metrics
- Identify risks/blockers and agree on mitigations
- Align on Sprint 8 Week 2 priorities, owners, and timelines

---

## 2) Agenda (suggested 45–60 mins)
| Time (mins) | Section | Owner |
|---:|---|---|
| 0–3 | Welcome, objectives, agenda | **Alex** |
| 3–10 | Sprint overview: scope, progress, highlights | **Alex** |
| 10–25 | Backend module: deliverables, API changes, reliability | **Bai** |
| 25–35 | Frontend module: UI/UX updates, performance, rollout status | **Chen** |
| 35–45 | QA module: test status, coverage, defect trends, release readiness | **Dana** |
| 45–55 | Risks/blockers review + decisions needed | **Alex (facilitator)** |
| 55–60 | Q&A + next steps | **All** |

> If you need a shorter meeting (30–40 mins), compress each module to ~7 mins and keep Q&A to 5 mins.

---

## 3) Presentation modules (by team member)

### 3.1 Alex — Sprint overview & facilitation (7–10 mins)
**Slides / talking points**
- Sprint 8 context: theme and Week 1 goals
- Week 1 delivered items (high-level bullets)
- “What changed” since sprint start (scope adjustments, priority shifts)
- Key metrics snapshot (availability, throughput, adoption) — *use the week’s exported metrics*
- Calls for attention: key risks, dependencies, decision points

**Inputs to reference**
- Sprint meeting notes
- Midpoint / weekly report
- Kanban snapshot

**Outputs / decisions to capture**
- Confirm “done” definition applied consistently
- Decide on Week 2 focus (top 3 priorities)
- Confirm release gates / rollout plan (if applicable)

---

### 3.2 Bai — Backend module (10–15 mins)
**Slides / talking points**
- Backend deliverables shipped this week (features + fixes)
- API changes / versioning impacts (include ADR highlights)
- Reliability: error trends, incident notes, mitigations
- Performance: request volume trends and any bottlenecks
- Upcoming backend work in Week 2 (scope + estimated impact)

**Inputs to reference**
- API request trend analysis
- Error event summary
- API versioning ADR
- Deploy changelog
- CI/build status (brief)

**Q&A seed questions (optional)**
- Any breaking-change risk for clients?
- Are we within error-budget / SLO boundaries?

---

### 3.3 Chen — Frontend module (8–12 mins)
**Slides / talking points**
- UX/UI changes delivered (demo-ready items)
- Performance improvements (load time, bundle size) if applicable
- Rollout / feature flag status
- Known issues and what’s planned for Week 2

**Inputs to reference**
- Sprint meeting notes
- Kanban snapshot
- Any UI-focused acceptance notes from QA

**Demo suggestions**
- 1–2 short flows only; keep demo under 3 minutes total.

---

### 3.4 Dana — QA module (8–12 mins)
**Slides / talking points**
- Test execution summary (what was run, what remains)
- Coverage snapshot and trend (unit/integration/e2e)
- Defect summary: new, reopened, critical
- Release readiness assessment + go/no-go criteria
- Week 2 test plan focus areas

**Inputs to reference**
- Test coverage report
- Daily report
- Defect / error summaries

---

## 4) Risks / blockers & mitigations (5–10 mins)
**Format**
- Top 3 risks (impact × probability)
- Owner + mitigation plan + due date
- Escalations / decisions needed today

**Inputs to reference**
- Risk assessment v2
- Midpoint report

---

## 5) Q&A (time-boxed)
### 5.1 Module Q&A (embedded)
- **2 mins** quick clarifications after each module (Alex/Bai/Chen/Dana)

### 5.2 Final Q&A (5–10 mins)
- **5 mins** general Q&A (All)
- **2–3 mins** parking-lot review (Alex)

---

## 6) Closing: next steps (2–3 mins)
- Confirm Week 2 priorities + owners
- Confirm dates: demos, release checkpoints, QA gates
- Action items recap (owner, due date)

---

## 7) Appendix (optional)
- Links to weekly artifacts (reports, changelog, ADRs, metrics export)
- KPI definitions / calculation notes
- Parking-lot questions

Created the Sprint Review presentation outline (Markdown) and saved it to:

- `day10/20260327_sprint8_week1_review_presentation_outline.md`
